prompt: Why is evaluating an AI system harder than testing a typical deterministic program?
choices: The same prompt can produce different wording each run, and there is rarely one single correct output string
AI systems never produce incorrect outputs, so there is nothing to test
It is impossible to measure latency or cost for AI systems
Deterministic programs cannot be tested at all

prompt: Which of the following are evaluation APPROACHES (ways to gather judgments), as opposed to metrics? (Select all that apply.)
A summarizer's outputs sometimes include details that never appeared in the source review. Which metric most directly captures this problem?
A PM wants to ship a prompt change but must avoid silently degrading quality. Which practices support safe, evidence-based iteration? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Rerun a golden-dataset eval suite after the change and keep it only if scores improve with no regression
Run the eval suite automatically as a regression gate before each release
Judge the change by how good it looks on two or three hand-picked demos
Track latency and cost alongside quality so a quality gain isn't hiding a speed or cost regression
